message:
fix(security): replace stub signature verification with real Ed25519 cryptography

Threshold signatures and impossible-default capability tokens now use
ed25519_dalek for signing and verification instead of SHA-256 hash
comparisons, closing the gap between scaffolding and production security:

- threshold_sig: sign() takes SigningKey + key_id instead of SimerKey;
  verify_signature() decodes hex public keys and signatures and calls
  verify_strict(); domain separation prefix "threshold_sig_verify_v1:"
  preserved
- impossible_default: HashSignatureVerifier replaced with
  Ed25519SignatureVerifier holding a VerifyingKey; with_default_verifier()
  renamed to with_ed25519_verifier(vk); all tests generate keypairs
  and sign with Ed25519
- threshold_signature_verification (integration test): test helpers
  generate deterministic Ed25519 keypairs from SHA-256 seeds
- check_impossible_default.py: updated struct name check from
  HashSignatureVerifier to Ed25519SignatureVerifier

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -207,7 +207,7 @@ def check_sha256() -> None:
 def check_signature_verifier() -> None:
     """Check SignatureVerifier trait."""
     _file_contains(IMPL, "pub trait SignatureVerifier", "trait: SignatureVerifier")
-    _file_contains(IMPL, "pub struct HashSignatureVerifier", "struct: HashSignatureVerifier")
+    _file_contains(IMPL, "pub struct Ed25519SignatureVerifier", "struct: Ed25519SignatureVerifier")
 
 
 def check_tests() -> None:
